curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 25 units, 25 chapters, and 1 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Explain the importance of chemistry in various disciplines.
Distinguish between different types of matter and their properties.
Apply principles of uncertainty and significant figures in measurements.
Utilize laws of chemical combination to predict reaction outcomes.
Calculate atomic and molecular masses using Dalton's theory.
Perform mole concept calculations and determine molar masses.
Compute percentage composition from chemical formulas.
Execute stoichiometric calculations for reactants and products.
Trace the discovery history of sub-atomic particles.
Compare historical atomic models with modern quantum mechanical concepts.
Analyze developments leading to Bohr's model for hydrogen.
Apply Bohr's theory to explain spectral lines of hydrogen.

competency_groups.title: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Foundational understanding of matter, measurement, and stoichiometry.
competencies: Explain the importance of chemistry in various disciplines.
Distinguish between different types of matter and their properties.
Apply principles of uncertainty and significant figures in measurements.
Utilize laws of chemical combination to predict reaction outcomes.
Calculate atomic and molecular masses using Dalton's theory.
Perform mole concept calculations and determine molar masses.
Compute percentage composition from chemical formulas.
Execute stoichiometric calculations for reactants and products.

competency_groups.title: Structure of Atom
competency_groups.description: Understanding the evolution and models of atomic structure.
competencies: Trace the discovery history of sub-atomic particles.
Compare historical atomic models with modern quantum mechanical concepts.
Analyze developments leading to Bohr's model for hydrogen.
Apply Bohr's theory to explain spectral lines of hydrogen.
Describe the transition from classical mechanics to quantum mechanical models.

competency_groups.title: Qualitative Analysis (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Identification techniques for ions in salts.
competencies: Identify one anion and one cation from a given salt sample using standard tests.

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Principles of electrochemical cells and energy conversion.
competencies: Differentiate between galvanic and electrolytic cells.
Apply the Nernst equation to calculate cell potentials under non-standard conditions.
Analyze conductance properties in electrolytic solutions.
Explain mechanisms of batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion processes.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Study of reaction rates and influencing factors.
competencies: Define rate of a chemical reaction and determine it experimentally.
Analyze factors influencing the rate of reactions including concentration, temperature, and catalysts.
Derive and apply integrated rate equations for zero and first-order reactions.
Explain collision theory in the context of molecular interactions.

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Elements (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Properties and applications of transition and inner-transition elements.
competencies: Correlate electronic configuration with position in the periodic table for d-block elements.
Describe general properties including oxidation states, magnetic behavior, and catalytic activity.
Explain characteristics of lanthanoids and actinoids.
Identify applications of transition metals in industrial processes.

competency_groups.title: Coordination Compounds (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Structure, bonding, and isomerism in coordination chemistry.
competencies: Apply Werner's theory to explain the structure of coordination compounds.
Use IUPAC nomenclature rules for naming complex ions.
Identify different types of isomerism (structural and stereoisomerism) in complexes.
Compare bonding theories including Valence Bond Theory, Crystal Field Theory, and Metal Carbonyl bonding.

competency_groups.title: Haloalkanes and Haloarenes (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Properties, preparation, and reactions of halogenated hydrocarbons.
competencies: Distinguish between haloalkanes and haloarenes based on structure and bonding.
Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules for polyhalogen compounds.
Predict products of substitution and elimination reactions in haloalkanes.
Analyze the nature of C-X bonds and their reactivity.

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols and Ethers (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Classification and properties of oxygen-containing organic compounds.
competencies: Differentiate between alcohols, phenols, and ethers based on functional groups.
Apply nomenclature rules for commercially important alcohols.
Analyze physical properties including boiling points and solubility trends.

competency_groups.title: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Reactivity and synthesis of carbonyl compounds.
competencies: Identify functional groups in aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids.
Predict preparation methods for these compounds from precursors.
Analyze physical properties and chemical reactions including nucleophilic addition.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 8: Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids

competency_groups.title: Amines (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Structure, properties, and synthesis of nitrogen-containing compounds.
competencies: Distinguish primary, secondary, and tertiary amines based on structure.
Apply nomenclature rules for substituted amines.
Predict preparation methods including reduction of nitro compounds and Hofmann bromamide reaction.

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Chemistry of carbohydrates, proteins, and nucleic acids.
competencies: Classify biomolecules into carbohydrates, proteins, vitamins, hormones, and enzymes.

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Phenomena at interfaces and colloidal systems.
competencies: Differentiate between physisorption and chemisorption mechanisms.

competency_groups.title: General Principles of Isolation (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Methods for extraction and purification of elements.
competencies: Explain principles of concentration, oxidation, reduction, electrolytic refining.
competency_groups.unit_name: General Principles and Processes of Isolation
competency_groups.chapter_name: General Principles and Processes of Isolation

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry: Basic Principles (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Fundamentals of organic structure, nomenclature, and mechanisms.
competencies: Represent structures using various notations including bond-line formulas.
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbons (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Classification and properties of aliphatic and aromatic hydrocarbons.
competencies: Distinguish between alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatics.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Theories of chemical bonding and molecular geometry.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach to predict bond formation.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Energy changes in chemical processes.
competencies: Define thermodynamic terms including enthalpy, entropy, and Gibbs energy.

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes.
competencies: Apply the law of mass action to calculate equilibrium constants.

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Oxidation-reduction processes and electrode potentials.
competencies: Identify oxidation numbers in complex molecules.

competency_groups.title: Classification of Elements (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Periodic trends and electronic configurations.
competencies: Explain the genesis of periodic classification.

competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Qualitative Analysis (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Detection of elements in organic compounds.
competencies: Detect nitrogen, sulphur, and chlorine in organic samples.

competency_groups.title: Project Work (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Scientific investigation and data analysis.
competencies: Design and execute a scientific project involving laboratory testing.

competency_groups.title: Practical Skills (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Laboratory techniques and safety.
competencies: Demonstrate basic laboratory skills including cutting, bending glass tubes.

competency_groups.title: Characterization (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Purification and melting/boiling point determination.
competencies: Determine physical constants like melting and boiling points.

competency_groups.title: pH Experiments (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Acid-base properties and indicators.
competencies: Determine pH of various solutions using universal indicator.

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Shifts (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Le Chatelier's principle application.
competencies: Observe shifts in equilibrium due to concentration changes.

competency_groups.title: Solutions (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Properties and concentration expressions.
competencies: Calculate concentrations using various units.

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Colloidal systems and adsorption.
competencies: Prepare lyophilic and lyophobic sols.

competency_groups.title: Chromatography (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Separation techniques.
competencies: Perform paper chromatography to separate pigments.

competency_groups.title: Organic Synthesis (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Preparation of organic compounds.
competencies: Synthesize acetanilide or other specified derivatives.

competency_groups.title: Functional Group Tests (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Identification of organic functional groups.
competencies: Perform characteristic tests for unsaturation, alcohols, phenols, etc.

competency_groups.title: Food Chemistry (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Analysis of food components.
competencies: Detect carbohydrates, fats, and proteins in food samples.

competency_groups.title: Redox Titration (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Determination of KMnO4 concentration.
competencies: Standardize KMnO4 solution using oxalic acid or Mohr's salt.

competency_groups.title: Qualitative Analysis (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Advanced ion detection.
competencies: Identify cations and anions in complex mixtures.

competency_groups.title: Project Work (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Investigatory projects.
competencies: Conduct a scientific investigation on food adulteration or fermentation.

learning_outcomes.topic: Foundational understanding of matter, measurement, and stoichiometry.
outcomes: Explain the importance of chemistry in various disciplines.
Distinguish between different types of matter and their properties.
Apply principles of uncertainty and significant figures in measurements.
Utilize laws of chemical combination to predict reaction outcomes.
Calculate atomic and molecular masses using Dalton's theory.
Perform mole concept calculations and determine molar masses.
Compute percentage composition from chemical formulas.
Execute stoichiometric calculations for reactants and products.

learning_outcomes.topic: Understanding the evolution and models of atomic structure.
outcomes: Trace the discovery history of sub-atomic particles.
Compare historical atomic models with modern quantum mechanical concepts.
Analyze developments leading to Bohr's model for hydrogen.
Apply Bohr's theory to explain spectral lines of hydrogen.
Describe the transition from classical mechanics to quantum mechanical models.
